rationale: The page provides specific details about the Sundarbans Tiger Reserve (STR) in West Bengal, including its recent expansion, size ranking relative to other reserves, geographical location, and bordering water bodies. The lower portion of the page is an advertisement for mapping classes.
cleanedText: West Bengal. Sundarbans Tiger Reserve (STR). Context: STR is now India's second largest after expansion of its area was approved by National Board of Wildlife (NBWL). - Now STR is just behind Andhra Pradesh's Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve in size. - Location: Coastal districts of West Bengal. - Only mangrove forest (with Bangladesh) housing a significant tiger population. - Borders: Bangladesh (east), Bay of Bengal (south), River Matla (west), Rivers Bidya & Gomdi (north-west). - Associated Designations: UNESCO World Heritage Site, CA|TS (Conservation Assured | Tiger Standards), MAB (Man and the Biosphere Programme).
